details.claim: Speculative decoding can reduce latency by up to 2-3 times compared to standard autoregressive decoding.
The verification phase uses a modified rejection sampling algorithm.
details.reason: Source [2] describes the verification process using a modified rejection sampling algorithm.

details.claim: Speculative decoding preserves the original output distribution of the target model.
The drafting phase generates a short sequence of candidate tokens (typically between 3 and 12).
details.reason: Source [1] specifies the typical length of the draft sequence.

details.claim: Speculative decoding is an inference-time optimization for autoregressive large language models (LLMs) that generates multiple tokens per decoding step instead of one.
details.reason: Source [3] describes speculative decoding as generating multiple tokens in parallel.

details.claim: The technique alternates between two phases: drafting and verification.
A smaller draft model proposes a sequence of candidate tokens, and the larger target model verifies them in a single forward pass through a modified rejection sampling scheme.
Speculative decoding can accelerate existing off-the-shelf models without retraining or architecture changes.
details.reason: Source [2] states that speculative decoding can be applied to existing models without modifications.

details.claim: Assisted generation is naturally designed for greedy decoding, but it can also be used with multinomial sampling.
details.reason: Source [5] discusses the use of assisted generation with both greedy and sampling-based methods.

details.claim: Assisted generation can deliver significant latency reductions in diverse settings, but it is not a silver bullet – you should benchmark it before applying it to your use case.
details.reason: Source [6] provides details on the benefits and limitations of assisted generation.

details.claim: Assisted generation requires access to an assistant model that is at least an order of magnitude smaller than your model.
Assisted generation can get up to 3x speedups in the presence of INT8 and up to 2x otherwise, when the model fits in the GPU memory.
details.reason: Source [6] provides specific latency reduction numbers.

details.claim: Assisted generation shines in input-grounded tasks, like automatic speech recognition or summarization.
details.reason: Source [6] lists input-grounded tasks as areas where assisted generation performs well.

details.claim: Speculative decoding is an optimization technique for large language models (LLMs) that aims to reduce inference latency by generating multiple tokens per decoding step.
The verification phase scores the entire draft sequence in one batched forward pass.
